explanation: The input requirement states that at the end of an inspiration phase, if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI, then an Inspiratory Pause should begin. To capture this, we first clarify the boolean expressions: 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' captures the end of the inspiration phase, 'inspiratoryPauseButton' means the Inspiratory Pause was set by GUI, and 'inspiratoryPause' marks the actual start of an Inspiratory Pause. Therefore, the requirement is: whenever (at any time) the end of the inspiratory phase happens and the Inspiratory Pause is requested, then in the next step an Inspiratory Pause must start. This fits template 41: G((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3)), meaning always when both conditions (end of inspiratory phase AND pause button) occur, inspiratory pause starts in the next state.

The natural language states: At the end of an inspiration phase, if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI, an Inspiratory Pause shall start. This means that whenever an inspiratory phase ends ("inspiratoryPhaseEnd"), and the user set the inspiratory pause ("inspiratoryPauseButton"), the system must enter inspiratory pause ("inspiratoryPause"). Therefore, we need to capture that always, if (the inspiration phase ends AND inspiratory pause is set by GUI) THEN inspiratoryPause will be true in the next step (i.e., it starts immediately after end of inspiration). This is a standard LTL pattern for such requirements and matches template 41: G((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3)), with bool_exp1 = inspiratoryPhaseEnd, bool_exp2 = inspiratoryPauseButton, and bool_exp3 = inspiratoryPause. N_DURATION is not used in this template and is returned as null.

The property expresses: Whenever in PSV Mode, at the end of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if the Inspiratory Pause has been set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), then inspiratoryPause becomes true in the next state. This corresponds closely with the requirement, since the 'end of an inspiration phase' is detected by inspiratoryPhaseEnd, the 'if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI' is captured by inspiratoryPauseButton, and the 'shall start' is captured by making inspiratoryPause true immediately after. The template is an implication (in PSV mode and at end of inspiration and GUI pause set) implies that next, inspiratoryPause holds.

The requirement is: At the end of an inspiration phase, if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI, an Inspiratory Pause shall start. This means that whenever 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' (end of inspiration) occurs, and the 'inspiratoryPauseButton' is pressed ('set by the GUI'), in the next moment, the system must enter 'inspiratoryPause'.

This is a cause-effect pattern that ties two inputs ('inspiratoryPhaseEnd' and 'inspiratoryPauseButton') to an outcome ('inspiratoryPause') in the next state. That is, globally, whenever the end condition and the button are both true, the next state must have the pause active.

Template 41 is 'G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3))', which is appropriate for this kind of guarded next-state effect. The selection of the expressions is:
bool_exp1 = inspiratoryPhaseEnd (end of inspiration)
bool_exp2 = inspiratoryPauseButton (button set by the GUI)
bool_exp3 = inspiratoryPause (pause becomes active)
This LTL specification states: at all times, if both the end of inspiratory phase and the inspiratory pause button are set, then in the next step, inspiratoryPause must be on.
bool_exp4, N_DURATION are not used in this template.

This LTL property expresses: Whenever (globally) 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' and 'inspiratoryPauseButton' are both true at the same moment (i.e., at the end of an inspiration phase and if the inspiratory pause is set by the GUI), then in the next state 'inspiratoryPause' must hold (i.e., an inspiratory pause shall start). This matches the requirement by explicitly tying the condition at the end of the inspiratory phase to the required outcome in the next step. All boolean expressions are valid and non-empty, derived from the atomic propositions and their descriptions.

The LTL property expresses that, always (globally), if the end of the inspiratory phase occurs (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) AND the Inspiratory Pause button has been set in the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), THEN in the next step (immediately following state), the Inspiratory Pause must be active (inspiratoryPause). This matches the natural language requirement that, upon end of an inspiration phase in PSV mode, if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI, an Inspiratory Pause must start.

The property states: At every moment (globally), if PSV Mode is active, an inspiration phase has just ended, and the Inspiratory Pause button is set by the GUI, then in the next state (i.e., immediately after inspiration phase end and button press), an Inspiratory Pause shall start. This closely follows the requirement that the Inspiratory Pause is to start at the end of inspiration if set in the GUI and PSV Mode is active.

The requirement states that in PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) Mode, at the end of an inspiration phase, if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI, an Inspiratory Pause must start. This is best captured using a response pattern: Globally, whenever the PSVMode is active AND the end of inspiratory phase occurs AND the inspiratoryPauseButton (set by GUI) is true, then in the next step, inspiratoryPause should be true (i.e., an Inspiratory Pause starts). Here, bool_exp1 = PSVMode & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton (the trigger); bool_exp3 = inspiratoryPause (the response that must hold in the next state). Template 31 is most suitable for this immediate reaction.

This LTL property specifies that whenever PSV mode is active and the inspiratory phase ends, if the user has set the Inspiratory Pause through the GUI (i.e., 'inspiratoryPauseButton' is true), then in the next step (immediately after the end of inspiration), the Inspiratory Pause must be initiated ('inspiratoryPause' becomes true). This directly follows the requirement: at the end of the inspiration phase in PSV mode, if the Inspiratory Pause has been requested, the pause must start.

The requirement specifies: at the end of an inspiration phase, if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI, an Inspiratory Pause shall start. This requires that globally (at all points in the execution), whenever both the end of the inspiratory phase occurs (inspiratoryPhaseEnd is true) and the pause button is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton is true), an inspiratory pause (inspiratoryPause) should start. Template 41 encodes the global implication: whenever bool_exp1 and bool_exp2 are both true, then bool_exp3 must follow. Here, bool_exp1 is inspiratoryPhaseEnd, bool_exp2 is inspiratoryPauseButton, and bool_exp3 is inspiratoryPause.
